### REQ-009: Auto-generated group landing page (`<group>/index.html`)

- **Status:** DONE
- **Added:** 2026-04-14
- **Completed:** 2026-04-14
- **Verified by:** Peter Froehlich
- **Depends on:** REQ-008
- **Description:**

**Problem:** With groups in place (REQ-007) and group/title metadata in the
manifest (REQ-008), visiting `https://material.professorfroehlich.de/mk4-26/`
with a valid `mk4-26` token currently produces a directory listing or a 404,
depending on the web server. Students would have to know and bookmark each
child project's URL individually.

**Solution:** Auto-generate a minimal static `index.html` at the root of each
group, listing all child projects with human-readable titles as links. One
token URL (`…/mk4-26/?token=...`) then serves as the single distribution link
for the whole group of co-deployed materials.

---

### 1. When the index page is regenerated

Matctl regenerates `<material>/<group>/index.html` on every operation that
affects the group's membership or any child's visible metadata:

| Operation | Triggers regeneration of |
|---|---|
| `course add <name> --group <g>` | `<g>/index.html` |
| `doc add <name> --group <g>` | `<g>/index.html` |
| `course remove <name>` (if grouped) | `<old-group>/index.html` |
| `doc remove <name>` (if grouped) | `<old-group>/index.html` |
| `course modify <name> --title "..."` (if grouped) | `<group>/index.html` |
| `doc modify <name> --title "..."` (if grouped) | `<group>/index.html` |
| `course modify <name> --group <new>` | `<old>/index.html` **and** `<new>/index.html` |
| `doc modify <name> --group <new>` | same |
| `group modify <g> --title "..."` | `<g>/index.html` |
| `group add <g>` | no-op on disk (no children yet) |
| `group remove <g>` | no-op on disk (must already be empty per REQ-008) |

Regeneration reads the current `projects.yml` and overwrites the target
`index.html` atomically.

---

### 2. Directory lifecycle

`<material>/<group>/` is created on demand:

- **Created** the first time the group gains a child (i.e. the first
  `course/doc add --group <g>` or `course/doc modify --group <g>` that
  produces a non-empty group). Matctl creates the directory and writes
  `index.html`.
- **Deleted** when the group becomes empty (the last child is removed or
  moved to a different group, or removed from the group via
  `modify --group ""`). Matctl removes `<group>/index.html` and the empty
  `<group>/` directory.
- **Never** created by `matctl group add` alone — an empty group has no
  on-disk footprint.

This keeps the invariant simple: **a `<group>/` directory in the working
tree exists if and only if the group has at least one child project.**

---

### 3. Page content and structure

The generated `index.html` is a minimal self-contained HTML5 document with
inline CSS. No external assets, no JavaScript, no Quarto render step.

Structure:

- `<title>` = group title (from manifest)
- `<h1>` = group title
- One or two sections:
  - **Courses** (heading + `<ul>` of `<li><a>`s) — shown only if the group
    has at least one `type: course` child
  - **Documents** (heading + `<ul>` of `<li><a>`s) — shown only if the group
    has at least one `type: doc` child
- Within each section, projects are listed in the order they appear in
  `projects.yml`.
- Each link:
  - `href="./<name>/"` (relative)
  - `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` (open in new tab, preserve
    the landing page)
  - Link text = the project's `title` from the manifest

Styling is intentionally minimal — legible system font, readable line-height,
modest max-width. Not branded: matctl has no access to the Quarto/SCSS
pipeline. A future requirement may add brand-consistent styling if needed.

---

### 4. CI deployment

The `material` repo's `publish.yml` workflow must deploy each
`<group>/index.html` in the repo to `httpdocs/<group>/index.html` on Netcup.

Implementation sketch (details deferred to the implementation plan):

- Enumerate `type: group` entries in `projects.yml`.
- For each group with a corresponding `<group>/index.html` present in the
  checkout, `scp` the file to `httpdocs/<group>/index.html` after the
  existing child-project deploy steps (so the directory exists).
- No Quarto render step for group landing pages.

The index page is covered by the `mk4-26` token scope via the Worker rules
already in place (REQ-007). No Worker changes.

---

### 5. Interaction with `matctl link`

The generated `<group>/index.html` is plain HTML; it does not reference
`_brand.yml` or `shared/`. `matctl link` / `unlink` are unaffected.

---

### 6. Documentation updates

- `docs/administration.md` — document the group landing page, its URL,
  regeneration triggers, and CI deploy step.
- `docs/authoring.md` — brief note: grouped materials get a shared landing
  page at `<group>/`; the token URL for the group points there.
- `CLAUDE.md` — mention the generated `<group>/index.html` in the
  repository-layout or matctl-CLI section as appropriate.

---

- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - [x] After `matctl group add mk4-26 --title "..."` alone, no `mk4-26/`
        directory exists in the working tree
  - [x] After `matctl course add digital-und-mikrocomputertechnik --group
        mk4-26 --title "..."`, `mk4-26/index.html` exists and lists that
        course under a "Courses" section
  - [x] After additionally `matctl doc add esp-survival-guide --group mk4-26
        --title "..."`, `mk4-26/index.html` lists the course under "Courses"
        and the doc under "Documents", in that order
  - [x] `matctl course modify <name> --title "New"` on a grouped course
        updates the link text in `<group>/index.html`
  - [x] `matctl group modify mk4-26 --title "New Group Title"` updates the
        `<h1>` and `<title>` of `mk4-26/index.html`
  - [x] `matctl course modify <name> --group mk4-27` regenerates both
        `mk4-26/index.html` (without that course) and `mk4-27/index.html`
        (with it)
  - [x] Removing the last child of a group deletes `<group>/index.html` and
        the `<group>/` directory from the working tree
  - [x] All generated links open in a new tab (have `target="_blank"` and
        `rel="noopener noreferrer"`)
  - [x] All generated links use relative paths (`./<name>/`)
  - [x] Project order within each section matches manifest order
  - [x] CI deploys `mk4-26/index.html` to `httpdocs/mk4-26/index.html` on
        Netcup
  - [x] Browser test: a student with a valid `mk4-26` token visits
        `https://material.professorfroehlich.de/mk4-26/`, sees the landing
        page, and clicks through to each child in a new tab without
        re-authenticating
  - [x] `docs/administration.md`, `docs/authoring.md`, and `CLAUDE.md`
        updated per §6

---

### 7. Deferred release steps — fire after REQ-009 is implemented

REQ-007, REQ-008, and REQ-009 are being batched into a single release. The
following steps are deliberately deferred until REQ-009 is complete. Do them
in order in one session.

**material-core:**

1. Bump `pyproject.toml` to the final version if not already `0.5.0`.
2. Commit everything on `main` (Phases 1–3 of each requirement).
3. Tag `v0.5.0` and push the tag:
   ```bash
   git tag v0.5.0
   git push origin v0.5.0
   ```

**material repo (`/home/peter/material/`):**

4. The following edits are **already on disk** (made during REQ-007 implementation):
   - `projects.yml` — `group: mk4-26` on both existing entries.
   - `.github/workflows/publish.yml` — `DEPLOY_PATH` resolution + `MATERIAL_CORE_REF: v0.5.0`.
   Commit and push them:
   ```bash
   git add projects.yml .github/workflows/publish.yml
   git commit -m "REQ-007/008/009: group rollout — mk4-26, grouped deploy, core v0.5.0"
   git push
   ```
   (Also commit the `mk4-26/index.html` generated by REQ-009 if it lands in `material/`.)

**Cloudflare Worker:**

5. Redeploy the Worker:
   - Open `material_core/cloudflare/worker.js` in this repo.
   - Paste the full contents into the Cloudflare dashboard editor for the
     `material.professorfroehlich.de` Worker.
   - Click **Deploy**.

**Manual acceptance run:**

6. Run the combined acceptance criteria from all three requirements:
   - REQ-007: A1–A11 (from `docs/plans/req-007-group-scope.md`).
   - REQ-008: criteria listed in `docs/requirements/REQ-008.md`.
   - REQ-009: criteria listed in `docs/requirements/REQ-009.md`.

---

- **Notes:**

- The page is static HTML, not Quarto. This avoids pulling the Quarto
  toolchain into matctl and keeps regeneration fast enough to run on every
  add/remove/modify without perceptible latency.
- The page is covered by the group's token scope — no additional Worker
  configuration. A student entering via `…/mk4-26/?token=...` hits the
  landing page, gets the `mk4-26`-scoped cookie, and can follow any link to
  a child without a second token.
- Links use `target="_blank"` so the landing page stays open as a "dashboard"
  while students open individual materials — matches the intent of
  distributing one URL per group.
- `subtitle` (on courses) is not displayed on the landing page. If a richer
  per-project description is needed later, add a `description` field to the
  manifest in a follow-up requirement.
- Ordering rule (courses before docs, manifest order within each) is
  deterministic and user-controllable: editing `projects.yml` by hand to
  reorder entries is supported, even though the preferred workflow is
  matctl-only.
